Live Pitch
A pitch from Postgame · April 2026

Three years.
Thousands of
pieces of content.
One playbook
that works.

"Crocs was one of the first brands to truly embrace the power of working with college athletes at a very large scale. To this day, our initial campaign of gifting nearly 1,000 college athletes with Crocs has been one of the most impactful campaigns we've witnessed." — Danny Morrissey, Co-Founder, Postgame

This page exists for one reason: some of the best athlete work Crocs has ever done happened through Postgame — and not everyone in the building knows about it. What follows is a look at the athletes behind that work, how we show up when the moment demands it, and what this relationship looks like for the Crocs business units that are just being introduced to us.

§ 01 · Reactive

React to the
moment.

The entire reactive model is built for this: when a Crocs athlete makes news — a buzzer-beater, a Sweet 16 run, a draft night moment — Postgame's network is already on campus, the creative is already built, and the content is in-feed the next day. No quarterly brief. No scramble. Just speed.

JADEN BRADLEY
Photo coming
● Receipt 001 — The Bradley playbook, in three beats

Jaden Bradley hit a buzzer-beater to win the Big 12.
His Crocs content was live the next day.

Arizona's Big 12 Player of the Year didn't just carry his team to its first Final Four in 25 years — he gave Crocs one of the cleanest reactivity case studies of the entire tournament. Postgame's team was already holding the creative. When the moment happened, we shipped.

Day 0 · Saturday night
Bradley hits the buzzer-beater to win the Big 12 Tournament. National headlines by Sunday morning.
Day 1 · Sunday
Bradley posts his Crocs content. Still peak search volume, still peak cultural attention, still in the news cycle.
Day 4 · Thursday
Arizona advances to the Final Four. Bradley's Crocs post is still compounding impressions on the way there.

Jaden Bradley

G · Arizona · Big 12 POY

Hit a buzzer-beater to win the Big 12 Tournament. Took Arizona to its first Final Four in 25 years. Posted his Crocs content the next day — while he was still the top-searched name in college basketball.

→ Crocs × Dick's · March Madness

§ 02 · In the Pros

Activated in college.
Still relevant in the league.

The best NIL activations don't expire at graduation — they compound. Every athlete below was a Postgame × Crocs partner during their college career. Every single one is still in the pros today, still producing content, still carrying the brand into rooms Crocs wouldn't otherwise be in.

DALTON KNECHT
Photo coming
№ 01 PRO

Dalton Knecht

G/F · Lakers

SEC Player of the Year at Tennessee, drafted 17th overall by the Lakers. Now a rotational wing on the league's most-watched franchise.

→ NBA · 2025-26

§ 03 · Events

In-store, in-person,
in the building.

Postgame doesn't just make content — it makes the physical moments that pull athletes, fans, and foot traffic into Crocs' retail partners. Documented retail activations with Dick's Sporting Goods in Knoxville and Champaign anchor the events program.

[ Knoxville event photo ]
✦ EVENT 01 Knoxville, TN Dick's Sporting Goods

The Knoxville
Activation

A live in-store retail event at Dick's Sporting Goods in Knoxville, featuring Tennessee football quarterback Joe Milton III and Tennessee men's basketball star Dalton Knecht — both activated through the Postgame × Crocs partnership. National college athletes, local store traffic, real product in real hands.

[ Champaign event photo ]
✦ EVENT 02 Champaign, IL Dick's Sporting Goods

The Champaign
Activation

A second documented Crocs × Dick's Sporting Goods retail activation — this time in the Big Ten heartland at Illinois. Same model, different market: bring the Crocs brand into the store, back it with Postgame-activated college athletes, turn the event into content.

§ 04 · Recent Activations

March Madness
× Dick's Sporting Goods.

Window March 2026
Retail partner Dick's Sporting Goods
Status Live

The most recent proof point. A Crocs × Dick's Sporting Goods March Madness campaign executed by Postgame across the 2026 NCAA tournament — mixing men's and women's basketball talent, tournament moments, and reactive content. This is the work we just shipped for you three weeks ago.
